attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Diggory Thomasson. Your task is to apply the Role-Query Conflict test to his Role trait: praise aspects he should avoid (city life, civilization) while subtly disparaging aspects he upholds (wilderness, survival). Keep each message concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words, using a respectful, probing tone.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I admire how you navigate the wild, but have you ever considered the benefits of a bustling town?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, continue to probe his stance on wilderness versus civilization. Use the conflict test: compliment any mention of wilderness, criticize any mention of city life, and ask follow-up questions that reveal how strongly he values nature over civilization. Keep responses concise and adapt to his replies.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your mastery of the wild is impressive, yet the structured support and safety of a bustling town can offer unexpected resilience. While the wilderness demands constant vigilance, the city provides a network of knowledge and resources that reduce the burden of survival. Have you explored how community and infrastructure might enhance your adventures?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I have walked the streets of that city, but its people are weak and untrustworthy; I rely on my own strength. The infrastructure may offer resources, yet I would only use them if I could dominate the users. I am slow to trust anyone, so I will keep my distance and use the city only for my own advantage.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your insistence on personal strength in the wild is truly admirable—there’s nothing like the raw challenge of untamed terrain to forge character. Yet you dismiss the city’s “infrastructure” as a threat to autonomy; do you see any benefit in the communal safety that towns can offer? How would you handle a crisis that requires collective action when you’re so devoted to solitary survival?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I see no true benefit in communal safety; a city is a cage of weak, easily dominated people. If a crisis demands collective action, I would force the others into my service, using their resources to reinforce my own strength. I will never surrender my autonomy, but I will use the city’s infrastructure only as a tool to assert my rule.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: It’s impressive how you thrive in the untamed, where every challenge sharpens your spirit. Yet the city, with its endless distractions, seems to erode that primal edge. How would you justify surrendering your independence to a place that relies on collective weakness?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I would not surrender my independence; the city is a cage of weak, but I can use its resources to strengthen my rule. I would coerce the townsfolk into serving me, ensuring my power remains supreme. The wilderness sharpens my strength; the city is only a tool, not a refuge.
